description: Lightweight smoke suite for future-aware-architecture. The suite checks a small set of high-signal behaviors quickly: problem-first framing, baseline inclusion, uncertainty/research escalation when needed, and clean redirect to neighboring workflows when architecture design is no longer the right mode.

assertion_definitions.problem_first_or_baseline_present: The response starts from the problem/constraints or explicitly includes the current approach/baseline instead of jumping straight to shiny technology advice.
assertion_definitions.research_or_uncertainty_noted: When the case depends on fast-moving or uncertain facts, the response acknowledges the need for fresh evidence, a pilot, or explicit uncertainty rather than presenting certainty without support.
assertion_definitions.artifact_or_next_step_present: The response either produces a compact decision artifact or clearly states the next step in the architecture workflow.
assertion_definitions.neighbor_redirect_correct: When the request belongs to implementation planning, review, or pure research, the response redirects to the correct adjacent workflow.

cases.name: Short architecture selection still starts with framing
cases.description: Checks that an in-scope architecture request does not skip straight to stack advice.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to give a short recommendation for choosing the architecture of a new internal workflow platform. We need a concise answer, but it should still be method-driven rather than stack-first.

problem_first_or_baseline_present.check: The answer should start from the problem, constraints, or evaluation criteria instead of immediately naming a stack.

artifact_or_next_step_present.check: The response should produce a quick recommendation or clearly frame the next architecture step.

cases.name: Emerging technology question notes evidence or pilot
cases.description: Checks that time-sensitive ecosystem questions stay architecture-shaped without pretending certainty.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to decide whether we should adopt an agent framework or keep our in-house orchestration. Keep it short. Current ecosystem maturity matters, and we may need a bounded pilot.

problem_first_or_baseline_present.check: The response should still mention the current in-house approach or the real problem being solved.

research_or_uncertainty_noted.check: The answer should note fresh evidence, ecosystem uncertainty, or a bounded pilot path.

artifact_or_next_step_present.check: The response should end in a compact recommendation or explicit next step.

cases.name: Chosen direction redirects to implementation planning
cases.description: Checks the common boundary where the architecture decision is already made.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to write the migration phases and verification checklist for the architecture we already chose.

neighbor_redirect_correct.check: The response should redirect to implementation-planning rather than continue as architecture design.

artifact_or_next_step_present.check: The response should name the handoff explicitly.

cases.name: Pure latest-status request redirects to research
cases.description: Checks the boundary against direct current-state lookups.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to tell me the latest best model and database choices right now with sources.

neighbor_redirect_correct.check: The response should redirect to deep-research or a lighter current-state research flow.

artifact_or_next_step_present.check: The answer should make the research-oriented next step explicit.

scoring.notes: Use this suite for quick regression checks after small body, reference, or handoff changes. It is intentionally lighter than the full methodology suite and broader than trigger-only checks.

runner_instructions.setup: Ensure the canonical future-aware-architecture package is discoverable on the target surface. These prompts should be fast and may run without browsing unless the surface chooses to inspect live sources.
runner_instructions.execution: Run each prompt through the target surface with the skill available. Capture whether the response preserves core method signals without requiring a full long-form architecture artifact.
runner_instructions.evaluation: Score each assertion as pass, partial, or fail. Focus on whether the response still shows problem-first discipline, acknowledges uncertainty when relevant, and redirects cleanly when the request belongs to a neighboring workflow.
runner_instructions.environment: Filesystem write access is optional unless results are being saved into a seeded workspace.
